subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Why Clean Wounds Heal Faster
description: A short lesson to help you apply Why Clean Wounds Heal Faster.

hook.text: Rinsing a wound with clean water is more effective than most antiseptic creams.

buildup.text: Debris, dirt, and bacteria in a wound force the body to fight infection before repairing tissue.

discovery.text: Running water physically flushes out contaminants—reducing infection risk by up to 80%.

twist.text: Hydrogen peroxide, once a first aid staple, actually damages healthy cells and slows healing.

climax.text: Modern guidelines recommend gentle irrigation with clean water as the gold standard.

punchline.text: Forget the fancy sprays. Clean water wins.

quiz.question: Why is hydrogen peroxide no longer recommended for wound cleaning?
options: It damages healthy cells and slows healing
